description: Bacteriophage lambda attP attachment site (POP'); 387 bp, source-verified 100% to AY048716.1. The phage-borne site recombined with the bacterial attB by lambda Int + IHF.

references.title: Viral integration and excision: structure of the lambda att sites
Integrative recombination of bacteriophage lambda: extent of the DNA sequence involved in attachment site function
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

references.title: Patterns of lambda Int recognition in the regions of strand exchange
Conditional-replication, integration, excision, and retrieval plasmid-host systems for gene structure-function studies of bacteria

label: lambda_attP
note: Bacteriophage lambda phage attachment site (attP, P-O-P'), the Int/IHF recombination substrate carrying the P and P' arms flanking the 15 bp common core. Identified as attP (NOT bacterial attB) — its source AY048716.1 is a CRIM integration plasmid (which carries attP), and it shares 192 bp of the P/P' arm region with the genomic lambda attP (a true attB would share only the ~15 bp core). Detailed sub-feature annotation (Int arm/core sites, IHF H/H1/H2, Xis) and functional_claims pending a re-run under the corrected attP identity.

provenance.sequence_source: AY048716.1 (CRIM plasmid pAH63, complete sequence; Haldimann & Wanner 2001 J Bacteriol 183:6384), complement strand positions 1545–1931, 100% identity over 387 bp
